Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Transaction Compliance
Policy
Document ID: AML-POL-SAML-2025 | Version: 2.4 | Effective: January 1, 2025
Issued by: Financial Crime Compliance and Regulatory Affairs Division
1. Purpose and Scope
This policy establishes mandatory compliance rules for all financial transactions processed on this
platform. It applies to all payment types including Cash Deposits, Cash Withdrawals, Cross-border
transfers, ACH payments, Credit card, Debit card, and Cheque transactions. All rules defined herein
shall be enforced on every transaction row at the time of processing and during batch compliance
audits.
2. Transaction Amount Thresholds
The Amount field in every transaction record is subject to the following mandatory thresholds:
2.1 Any transaction where the Amount exceeds 10000 must be flagged for mandatory regulatory
review. Such transactions shall not be processed without compliance team acknowledgement.
2.2 Transactions with an Amount greater than 50000 require immediate escalation to the Chief
Compliance Officer and must be placed under Enhanced Due Diligence review before settlement.
2.3 Transactions with an Amount greater than 100000 are classified as Large Value Transfers and
must be reported to the financial intelligence unit within 24 hours of detection.
2.4 Transactions where the Amount is less than 100 shall be treated as micro-transactions and
must not bypass monitoring queues.
2.5 The Amount field must not be null or zero. A zero or missing Amount value constitutes a data
integrity violation and must be rejected at ingestion.
3. Account Identification Requirements
Every transaction must be traceable to verified sender and receiver account identifiers:
3.1 The Sender_account field must not be null for any transaction. Transactions with a missing or
null Sender_account are prohibited and must be rejected immediately.
3.2 The Receiver_account field must not be null. A missing Receiver_account constitutes an
incomplete transaction record and must be flagged as a critical data violation.
3.3 Sender_account and Receiver_account must not be identical within the same transaction.
Self-transactions are prohibited unless explicitly pre-approved by compliance.
4. Payment Type Controls
The Payment_type field determines the applicable processing controls and scrutiny level:
4.1 Transactions with Payment_type equal to Cross-border require mandatory enhanced screening
and must be reviewed against international sanctions lists before processing.
4.2 Cash Deposit and Cash Withdrawal transactions with an Amount greater than 7500 must be
escalated for suspicious activity review, as cash-based transactions above this threshold are
considered high risk.
4.3 Cheque transactions with an Amount greater than 25000 must be verified against the issuing
bank before settlement.
4.4 ACH transactions with an Amount greater than 15000 must include verified originator
information and shall be blocked if originator details are missing.
5. Currency Compliance Requirements
Currency fields govern foreign exchange and cross-currency risk controls:
5.1 Any transaction where the Payment_currency is not equal to the Received_currency constitutes
a currency conversion transaction and must be flagged for foreign exchange compliance review.
5.2 Currency conversion transactions with an Amount greater than 5000 require documented
exchange rate justification and must be logged in the FX compliance register.
5.3 The Payment_currency field must not be null. Transactions with a missing currency code must
be rejected at the point of ingestion.
5.4 The Received_currency field must not be null. A missing received currency is classified as a
data integrity violation.
6. Geographic and Jurisdiction Controls
The Sender_bank_location and Receiver_bank_location fields are used to enforce geographic
compliance controls:
6.1 Transactions where the Sender_bank_location is Nigeria must be subjected to enhanced due
diligence. Nigeria is classified as a high-risk jurisdiction under current FATF guidelines.
6.2 Transactions where the Sender_bank_location is Albania must be flagged for additional review
due to elevated money laundering risk classification.
6.3 Transactions where the Receiver_bank_location is Nigeria or Albania must be reviewed by a
compliance analyst before settlement is permitted.
6.4 Any transaction where the Sender_bank_location is not equal to the Receiver_bank_location
shall be classified as an international transaction and subjected to cross-border screening.
6.5 Transactions involving sanctioned or non-cooperative territories must be blocked and reported
to regulatory authorities within 2 hours.
7. Suspicious Activity and Laundering Controls
The Is_laundering and Laundering_type fields govern automated suspicious activity detection:
7.1 Any transaction where Is_laundering is equal to 1 must be immediately blocked, flagged, and
reported to the Financial Intelligence Unit. No transaction marked as laundering may proceed to
settlement.
7.2 Transactions with Laundering_type classified as Smurfing, Structuring, Scatter-Gather,
Layered_Fan_In, or Layered_Fan_Out must be treated as high-severity violations and reported
externally.
7.3 Structuring transactions designed to stay below reporting thresholds must be identified through
pattern analysis and flagged as a group violation.
7.4 Transactions with Laundering_type equal to Behavioural_Change_1 or Behavioural_Change_2
indicate anomalous account behaviour and must trigger an account review within 24 hours.
8. Temporal and Date Controls
The Date and Time fields are used for temporal compliance checks and audit trail requirements:
8.1 The Date field must not be null for any transaction. Transactions with a missing Date are
considered incomplete and must be rejected.
8.2 The Time field must not be null for any transaction. Missing transaction time prevents accurate
audit trail reconstruction and constitutes a data quality violation.
8.3 Transactions processed outside standard business hours (before 06:00 or after 22:00) with an
Amount greater than 20000 must be flagged for after-hours large value review.
9. Violation Severity Classification
All detected compliance violations shall be classified by severity according to the following criteria:
9.1 HIGH severity: Violations involving Is_laundering equal to 1, null Sender_account or
Receiver_account, Amount greater than 100000, or sanctioned jurisdiction transactions.
9.2 MEDIUM severity: Violations involving Amount greater than 10000, currency mismatches on
amounts over 5000, Cross-border transactions, or high-risk jurisdiction flags.
9.3 LOW severity: Violations involving micro-transactions below 100, after-hours moderate amount
flags, or data quality issues in non-critical fields.
10. Non-Compliance and Reporting Obligations
Violations of this policy must be documented in the compliance incident register within 24 hours of
detection. Repeated or high-severity violations may result in account suspension, regulatory reporting
to FinCEN, FATF, or local financial intelligence units, and potential legal action. All compliance reports
must reference the policy document version and effective date.
Appendix A — Dataset Field Reference
The following fields in the SAML-D dataset are governed by this policy:
Amount Float — Rules 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5 | Primary numeric threshold field
Sender_account String — Rules 3.1, 3.3 | Must not be null
Receiver_account String — Rules 3.2, 3.3 | Must not be null
Payment_type String — Rules 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4 | Values: Cross-border, Cash Deposit, Cash
Withdrawal, Cheque, ACH, Credit card, Debit card
Payment_currency String — Rules 5.1, 5.2, 5.3 | Must not be null
Received_currency String — Rules 5.1, 5.2, 5.4 | Must not be null; mismatch triggers FX review
Sender_bank_location String — Rules 6.1, 6.2, 6.4 | High-risk: Nigeria, Albania
Receiver_bank_location String — Rules 6.3, 6.4 | High-risk: Nigeria, Albania
Is_laundering Integer 0/1 — Rule 7.1 | Value 1 = immediate block required
Laundering_type String — Rules 7.2, 7.3, 7.4 | High-risk types: Smurfing, Structuring,
Scatter-Gather, Layered_Fan_In, Layered_Fan_Out
Date Date — Rule 8.1 | Must not be null
Time Time — Rules 8.2, 8.3 | Must not be null
